page: Microsoft Azure E64-32as_v7 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the Easv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 16 physical cores and 32 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The CPU cache configuration includes 48 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates top-tier single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression scoring in the top 10%. Multi-core performance is also strong, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng multi-core, bzip3 multi-threaded decompression, and PassMark CPU tests. Memory performance is mixed; while the PassMark memory score ranks in the top 25%, memory bandwidth for both small and large blocks remains in the middle 50%.

The server shows a clear performance tradeoff in large-scale computational tasks. While LLM text generation for a small 135M model reaches the top 10%, text generation speed for a large 70B model falls into the bottom 10%, reflecting the lack of hardware acceleration. Given the high memory density of 512.0 GB RAM alongside 32 vCPUs, this server is suited for memory-intensive workloads such as database operations, caching layers, and memory-bound enterprise applications, rather than large-scale AI model inference.
description: Microsoft Azure E64-32as_v7 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server from the Easv7 family, powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running on Microsoft Hyper-V. It features 32 vCPUs, 16 physical cores, and 512.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high resource density of 16.0 GB per core. The server lacks local storage and GPUs but is Premium Storage capable. Benchmarks indicate top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong multi-core capabilities, though memory bandwidth and database operations sit in the middle 50%. A notable tradeoff is its poor performance in large-scale LLM text generation, where it falls into the bottom 10%. This server is best suited for memory-intensive enterprise applications, caching layers, and database workloads that benefit from high memory capacity relative to compute resources.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E64-32as_v7 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring 32 vCPUs, 512.0 GB RAM, and a 4.5 GHz AMD EPYC processor, delivering top-tier single-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E64-32as_v7 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server with 32 vCPUs, 512.0 GB RAM, and a 4.5 GHz AMD EPYC processor.
tagline: An x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring 512 GB RAM and a dedicated 4.5 GHz AMD EPYC processor for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores running at 4.5 GHz.
High memory capacity of 512.0 GB RAM providing 16.0 GB per core.
Top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong multi-core processing capabilities.
No local storage or GPU included, but supports Premium Storage.
Suited for memory-intensive databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications.
